overview: Strixhaven is a spells-matter set built around five distinct magical colleges. The format introduces the 'Learn' and 'Lesson' mechanic, effectively giving you an extra hand in your sideboard. It is a slower, high-synergy format where casting targeted spells and managing graveyard resources are vital for victory.

mechanics.name: Magecraft
mechanics.description: Triggers whenever you cast or copy an instant or sorcery spell.
mechanics.draft_tip: Prioritize cheap cantrips and combat tricks to trigger Magecraft multiple times in a single turn.

mechanics.name: Learn & Lesson
mechanics.description: Learn allows you to put a Lesson card from your sideboard into your hand or discard a card to draw a card.
mechanics.draft_tip: Draft at least 3-4 Lessons (sideboard cards) early so your Learn cards always have high-impact targets.

mechanics.name: Ward
mechanics.description: A keyword that counters spells or abilities unless the opponent pays an additional mana cost.
mechanics.draft_tip: Ward makes your high-cost bombs much harder to remove; use it to protect your late-game threats.

archetypes.name: Silverquill: Repartee Aggro
archetypes.description: Inspire your creatures with words of affirmation or hurl cutting critiques at enemies. Get rewarded for casting targeted spells while pressing the attack.
archetypes.strategy: Go aggressive. Use +1/+1 counters and targeted 'buff' spells to trigger Magecraft and finish the opponent before they stabilize.

archetypes.name: Prismari: Opus Spellcasting
archetypes.description: Load up your deck with instants and sorceries! Fire off cheap spells or get a showstopping bonus from spending five or more mana on your opus.
archetypes.strategy: Survive the early game with cheap interaction, then cast massive 5+ CMC spells to swing the game in your favor.

archetypes.name: Witherbloom: Lifegain Swarm
archetypes.description: Build up an army of Pests and other creatures to gain lots of life and overwhelm opponents. Steady lifegain will also infuse your spells with boosted effects.
archetypes.strategy: Generate Pest tokens. Use them as sacrifice fodder for value or as a way to gain life and trigger 'lifegain matters' cards.

archetypes.name: Lorehold: Flashback Excavation
archetypes.description: Fill your graveyard and then dig it back up again. When cards leave your graveyard, you'll uncover the power for history-making plays.
archetypes.strategy: Focused on the graveyard. Use recursion and effects that trigger when cards leave the yard to generate late-game value.

archetypes.name: Quandrix: Incremental Value
archetypes.description: Grow your creatures with counters as you cast bigger and bigger spells! Accelerate your mana and attack with huge creatures.
archetypes.strategy: Ramp to 8 lands as quickly as possible. Quandrix cards become significantly more powerful once you reach the '8 lands' threshold.

archetypes.name: Withered Knowledge
archetypes.description: A grindy control deck that uses self-mill and reanimation to outlast the opponent.
archetypes.strategy: Combine Silverquill's removal with Witherbloom's graveyard recursion to create a deck that never runs out of threats.

archetypes.name: Elemental Ramp
archetypes.description: Uses Prismari's big spells and Quandrix's ramp to cast massive Elemental threats.
archetypes.strategy: Ramp hard into 5-mana and 7-mana Elementals. This deck relies heavily on having a good mix of Learn cards and Lessons.

archetypes.name: Tokens & Counters
archetypes.description: Combines Witherbloom's token generation with Silverquill's +1/+1 counter support.
archetypes.strategy: Go wide with tokens and use Quandrix-style scaling to make your board state overwhelming for ground-based decks.

archetypes.name: Tempo Skies
archetypes.description: Evasive flyers protected by blue bounce and white protection spells.
archetypes.strategy: The 'anti-meta' deck. While others ramp or setup graveyards, you fly over and use tempo spells to keep them off-balance.

archetypes.name: Aggro Burn
archetypes.description: Fast-paced aggression using black's drain effects and red's direct damage.
archetypes.strategy: Use cheap creatures and burn spells to lower the opponent's life total quickly. Learns into damage-dealing Lessons to finish the job.

general_tips: Environmental Sciences is a high-priority Lesson; it ensures you hit your land drops in a format that rewards high-mana spells.
Don't main-deck Lessons unless they are extremely high impact; it's almost always better to keep them in the sideboard for Learn triggers.
Magecraft triggers on COPIED spells too. Spells with 'Storm' or Prismari copy effects provide double value.
The 5-mana hybrid Pledgemages (like Silverquill Pledgemage) are very flexible and should be picked highly for any deck in those colors.
Keep track of your graveyard for Lorehold strategies; exiling the wrong card can shut off your recursion engines.
